transcript:
Agent: Authorizations, hold please.
[4 minute hold]
Agent: Okay what do you need.
Caller: Hi, I've been waiting on an authorization for my spinal cord stimulator procedure. My surgeon's office said it was submitted three weeks ago.
Agent: What's your name.
Caller: Linda Parsons.
Agent: Date of birth.
Caller: February 14th, 1961.
Agent: Okay I see it. It's approved, you're good to go.
Caller: Oh that's great! So the procedure can be scheduled?
Agent: Yeah it's approved.
Caller: Should I call the surgeon's office to let them know, or will you contact them?
Agent: They'll get a fax. Look I have a lot of calls, is there anything else?
Caller: I just want to make sure — the authorization is for the spinal cord stimulator trial, right? Not just the consultation?
Agent: It's approved, I already told you that.
Caller: Okay but I'm just asking which procedure—
Agent: Ma'am, it's approved. I need to move on.
Caller: Okay fine. Thank you.
Agent: Mm-hm.
[Call ends]

[NOTE FOR QA: Internal records show this authorization is actually still PENDING review, not approved. The agent misread the status.]
